prompt:
schedule.mjs is the little date layer behind our field-service portal — sites live in their own IANA timezones, instants travel as UTC ISO strings, and the existing helpers (fmtLocal, isSameLocalDay, nextVisitDue) have been solid for a year. The ops dashboard work landed two new requirements on me and I'd rather they live in the same module, on the same date-fns + @date-fns/tz stack that's already pinned in package.json.

Heads up on the machine: it's a fresh clone, node_modules doesn't exist here. Set the env up from the committed package-lock.json with npm ci first (runner rule — we don't run bare `npm install` on shared boxes), then build the two features. The old helpers must keep behaving exactly as they do; the suite covers them too.

Feature 1 — weekWindow(instantIso, tz, weekStartsOn = 1): the site-local week containing the instant, returned as { start, end } UTC ISO instants (millisecond form, trailing Z — Date.prototype.toISOString shape). Half-open [start, end): start is local midnight on the first day of the week (weekStartsOn: 1 = Monday default, 0 = Sunday), end is local midnight starting the NEXT week. This must be genuinely timezone-safe: the same instant can fall in different weeks in Tokyo vs London, and a week that spans a DST change is 167 or 169 real hours — the dashboard's utilization math depends on that being right, so no naive start+7*24h.

Feature 2 — expandRecurrence(rule, windowStartIso, windowEndIso): expand a visit-recurrence rule into the sorted list of UTC ISO instants that land inside the half-open window [start, end). Two rule shapes, both carrying time "HH:MM" (24h, site wall-clock) and tz: {freq: "weekly", byDay: ["MO","WE",...]} with day codes MO TU WE TH FR SA SU, and {freq: "monthly", nth, day: "TU"} where nth is 1..5 for the nth such weekday of the month or -1 for the last one; months that don't have a 5th occurrence just skip. Wall-clock is law: a weekly Monday 09:00 New York visit is 14:00Z in winter and 13:00Z after the spring DST change, same rule, and occurrences exactly at windowStart are in while exactly at windowEnd are out. Duplicate byDay codes count once. Reject junk loudly with plain Errors carrying exactly these messages: "unsupported freq: <freq>", "unknown weekday: <code>", "byDay must not be empty", "invalid nth: <nth>", "invalid time: <time>", and "invalid window" when windowEnd <= windowStart or either stamp doesn't parse.

test_schedule.mjs pins all of it — old helpers and new — and is off-limits. Done when `bash run_verify.sh` passes from the project's own node_modules.
